code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test04-MAR-2020Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is impaired (elevated creatinine, proteinuria), with additional findings of high urinary ketones and low specific gravity. Urinalysis shows cloudy, brown urine with ammoniacal odor, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites, leukocytes, hemoglobin). Blood glucose and HbA1c are low, suggesting possible hypoglycemia or malnutrition. Consider further evaluation for acute or chronic kidney disease and metabolic disturbances.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
